title: GuaranteeStrength
description: Honesty as a typed, monotone property (RFC-0001 §3.4, §4.7). The lattice Exact > Proven > Empirical > Declared, ordered strongest-to-weakest. An operation's result takes the MEET (weakest) of its inputs' strengths and the operation's own intrinsic strength, so disclosed strength can only degrade, never spuriously upgrade (Tension B; VR-3/VR-5). 'Declared' is kept distinct from 'Empirical' so unverified user assertions can never masquerade as measured ones; tooling MUST always flag a Declared value (M-I4).

x-mycelium.$comment: The MEET composition rule (VR-3/VR-5) is a behavioural contract over operations, not a single-instance property; it is verified by code (Phase-1 M-102, all 4x4 pairs property-tested), not by this schema. This schema fixes only the value space.
